calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-08-2026 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Oct-13-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-17-2026 Continued To Jul-27-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-17-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: SANCTIONS.

The court orders plaintiff Reem Bitar to pay $500 in sanctions to this court by October 17, 2025.

On August 28, 2025, the court issued an order denying Bitar's motion to compel defendant American Honda Motor Co. to make further responses to her requests for production of documents. In its order, the court concluded that Bitar's motion lacked merit because Honda's responses complied with its Code obligations and Bitar's motion did not show specific facts showing good cause justifying the discovery sought by the demand. Honda did not seek sanctions, but the court ordered Bitar to show cause why she should not pay a sanction to the court pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure, section 2031.310, subdivision (h).

The court has considered Bitar's response to the OSC and now orders the sanction. Bitar's response declares that Honda's RFP responses were deficient because Bitar knows certain documents to exist but Honda stated they did not exist. (Ardi Dec. para. 8.) The problem is that this statement, like counsel's statements to the same effect at oral argument, appeared nowhere in the motion itself. Instead Bitar's motion to compel contained broad and seemingly boilerplate assertions that all of Honda's objections were without merit. The motion did not engage at all with the actual controversy between the parties. Parties cannot expect to prevail on discovery motions based on arguments they do not make in the motion, and it is not a useful expenditure of court time and resources to review and adjudicate motions that do not set out the actual issues in controversy and merely advance hypothetical arguments. The court concludes that Bitar's motion lacked substantial justification.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
